title: DocumentReference Object
description: Represents a reference to a document from another document.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getLatestVersion
methods.description: Updates the reference to use the latest version. This is only useful when the isOutOfDate property is true.

properties.name: dataFile
properties.description: The dataFile on A360 that this object references.

properties.name: isOutOfDate
properties.description: Indicates if this reference is out of date, meaning that the reference is not referencing the latest version.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentDocument
properties.description: The document that is doing the referencing and owns this reference.

properties.name: referencedDocument
properties.description: The document currently open in Fusion that this object references.

properties.name: version
properties.description: Gets and sets the version of the dataFile on A360 that this document currently represents. Setting this property will cause all occurrences referencing this document to update to that version.

classType.title: DocumentReference.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

getLatestVersion.title: DocumentReference.getLatestVersion Method
getLatestVersion.description: Updates the reference to use the latest version. This is only useful when the isOutOfDate property is true.
getLatestVersion.return_description: Returns true if getting the latest version was successful.

dataFile.title: DocumentReference.dataFile Property
dataFile.description: The dataFile on A360 that this object references.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.dataFile

isOutOfDate.title: DocumentReference.isOutOfDate Property
isOutOfDate.description: Indicates if this reference is out of date, meaning that the reference is not referencing the latest version.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.isOutOfDate

isValid.title: DocumentReference.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.isValid

objectType.title: DocumentReference.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.objectType

parentDocument.title: DocumentReference.parentDocument Property
parentDocument.description: The document that is doing the referencing and owns this reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.parentDocument

referencedDocument.title: DocumentReference.referencedDocument Property
referencedDocument.description: The document currently open in Fusion that this object references.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.referencedDocument

version.title: DocumentReference.version Property
version.description: Gets and sets the version of the dataFile on A360 that this document currently represents. Setting this property will cause all occurrences referencing this document to update to that version.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = documentReference_var.version

# Set the value of the property.
documentReference_var.version = propertyValue
